question: In a KCNA consumer group with 4 consumers consuming from a topic with 8 partitions, one consumer crashes. Describe the rebalancing process step by step. How does the group coordinator detect the failure? What happens to in-flight messages the crashed consumer had polled but not committed? Write a minimal consumer that handles the rebalance event by committing offsets before the partition is revoked.
answer: The group coordinator detects failure via session timeout and triggers a rebalance. All surviving consumers rejoin; the first responder becomes leader and computes a new partition assignment using the configured assignor. Orphaned partitions are redistributed. Uncommitted in-flight messages from the crashed consumer are reprocessed from the last committed offset, providing at-least-once delivery. Consumers detect rebalance on the next poll() call and must commit offsets before resuming.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  A[Consumer C1 Crashes] --> B{Coordinator detects via heartbeat timeout}
  B --> C[Trigger group rebalance]
  C --> D[Surviving consumers rejoin with JoinGroup]
  D --> E[Coordinator selects leader from first JoinGroup response]
  E --> F[Leader computes assignment for 8 partitions / 3 consumers]
  F --> G[Leader sends SyncGroup with full assignment]
  G --> H[Coordinator distributes assignment to each consumer]
  H --> I[Consumers fetch from last committed offset]
  I --> J[Processing resumes on new partitions]
  style A fill:#f96,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
  style H fill:#9cf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
